rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SHAW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 19, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Indian Head Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed March 29. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SHAW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 19, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Aerojet-General Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the fifth cause of action (premises owner/contractor liability) and taken off calendar as to the first through fourth and sixth through twentieth causes of action. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Adjudication of the first through fourth and sixth through twentieth causes of action is off calendar as moot because those causes of action are not alleged against defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CARROLL WILLIAM PEIL et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 19, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4, Defendant Nibbi Bros, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence (survival and wrongful death) and contractor liability, granted as to products liability (survival and wrongful death) and taken off calendar as to punitive damages. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mrs. Peil was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Carroll Peil¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to successfully shift its burden by negating an element of plaintiffs¿ claim. Mr. D¿Ercole did not have sufficient foundation to conclude that Carroll Peil was never employed by Nibbi Bros., Inc. Plaintiffs¿ objections to paragraph 8 of Mr. D¿Ercole¿s declaration are sustained. The remainder of his declaration merely creates a triable issue when read in conjunction with Carroll Peil¿s deposition testimony. Neither does Carroll Peil¿s Social Security statement of earnings successfully negate an element of plaintiffs¿ claim. As to products liability, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant was a manufacturer, supplier or distributor of products making it liable under a theory of strict liability. Plaintiffs have dismissed their claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY ROMAN SR. et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 19, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6, Defendant Albay Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Roman was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of Mr. Roman and Mr. Ay are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. rulings.Case Title: HUBERT WELCH VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 19, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 8, Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect), denied as to issue 4 (punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). Defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs have dismissed their claim for false representation. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES MCPEEK VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Notice Of Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 19, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 9, Defendant T.F.E. Corp.¿s motion to strike the amendment substituting defendant¿s name for a Doe defendant is denied. Plaintiff¿s complaint states sufficient allegations of alternate entity liability between T.F.E. Corp. and Lane Buick-Pontiac, Inc. The court did not consider any of the extrinsic evidence submitted by either side. Neither side properly requested judicial notice of such evidence and, even if they had, none of the evidence is the proper subject for the court to take judicial notice. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
